behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure can come from real speech-model behavior on multi-speaker audio (speaker diarization/label retention), which a mock transcript can hide. The boundary is not just API wiring; it is model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production speech-to-text API, then run the normal summarization step, and assert attendee attributions in the structured summary map to the speaker labels present in the transcript.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This is extraction/attribution quality from an LLM. Deterministic mocks or canned outputs can pass while real model outputs omit assignees or tasks.
behaviors.test_description: Use real meeting transcripts with known task assignments and call the real LLM summarization path; assert each attendee with an assigned task in ground truth appears with at least one action item in the output.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model’s semantic extraction and section placement. Mocked model responses would hide real misclassification/format-placement failures.
behaviors.test_description: Send real transcripts containing explicit decision statements through the real summarization model and assert those decisions are present under the structured `decisions` field, not only in freeform narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration: duration detection, segmentation boundary logic, ordered processing, and merge behavior across modules and filesystem. Pure unit mocks can miss cross-module state/ordering issues, but real model quality is not the core claim.
behaviors.test_description: Run the segmentation+sequential-processing pipeline with a >60-minute fixture audio (or synthetic equivalent) using real local file handling and deterministic stubs for STT/LLM responses per segment; assert correct segment count/order, sequential calls, and merged structured output shape/content continuity.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end outcome across multiple real agentic steps (STT then LLM summarization) plus pipeline orchestration/delivery. Single-step tests cannot expose chained failure modes or handoff breakage.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end run on a real meeting recording: upload, real STT call, real LLM summarization, and final delivery path; assert successful completion, valid structured schema, and no human retry/manual correction path used.
